appliedUpdates.description: Added test:coverage command (npx jest --coverage) referenced by feature verification steps but missing from services.yaml

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a concrete example for safe sql.js IN-clause patterns when using internal constants, to clarify the 'always use parameterized queries' convention.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: F002-BE used string-interpolated status values in SQL IN clauses, and F001-UI bypassed the dedicated dashboard:* IPC endpoints (breaking frontend/backend abstraction).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: backend-worker SKILL.md should more strongly emphasize updating electron.d.ts after adding IPC handlers, with a mandatory checklist step.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: F002-BE added dashboard IPC handlers to main.ts and preload.ts but did not update electron.d.ts, leaving renderer consumers without type declarations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: frontend-worker SKILL.md should enforce the agent-browser manual verification step more strictly; consider making it a required handoff field rather than optional.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: F001-UI handoff showed interactiveChecks: [] and no component tests were added, despite the skill explicitly requiring manual verification with agent-browser.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: test-automation-worker SKILL.md should include a mock recipe for sql.js + localDatabaseManager + filesystem reads to enable testing of database aggregation functions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: F003-TEST worker cited complexity of setting up in-memory sql.js databases as the reason for not testing getPendingApprovals and getApprovalCounts, leaving core logic with no test coverage.

rejectedObservations.observation: F001-UI did not leverage new services beyond vite-dev-server
rejectedObservations.reason: Not actionable; no service gap was identified.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml already includes necessary test/lint/typecheck commands (F002-BE)
TDD not applicable for F003-TEST since it was a pure test feature for already-implemented code
rejectedObservations.reason: Not a shared-state issue; worker handled this appropriately.
